One-page cheat sheet. Print or save to PDF for the morning of the interview: Ctrl/Cmd + P → Save as PDF, portrait, margins “Default”, and enable “Background graphics” so the colour accents print. It’s tuned to fit a single A4 page.

Java 21 & Spring — Interview Cheat Sheet

Tudor / Sagarsoft · JDK 21 · Spring Boot 3.5
your edge: 20 yrs ETL → modern Java

AThe 7 named topics — one-liners

Class/ObjectBlueprint vs instance. == reference, .equals() value. Override equals ⇒ override hashCode. Data structsList=ordered/dupes, Set=unique, Map=key→value. Default ArrayList/HashMap. HashMaphashCode→bucket→walk chain w/ equals. O(1) avg; long chain→tree (Java 8). Func iface1 abstract method (SAM); target of a lambda. Function/Predicate/Consumer/Supplier. RESTStateless HTTP; method=intent, JSON in/out, status code = outcome. Java 21Virtual threads, pattern-matching switch + record patterns, sequenced collections. SQL indexB-tree ⇒ O(log n) not full scan. Cost: slower writes. Verify with EXPLAIN ANALYZE.

JAVAVersion feature map

8lambdas, Streams, Optional, java.time, method refs
10var (local type inference)
14/15switch expressions, text blocks
16records, pattern-matching instanceof
17sealed classes (LTS)
21virtual threads, switch patterns + record patterns, sequenced collections (LTS)
21*structured concurrency = preview (say so)

P1Streams = your ETL pipeline

source → lazy intermediate ops → 1 terminal op

// filter→map→collect (GROUP BY style)
emps.stream()
  .filter(e -> e.salary() > 100000)
  .map(Employee::name)
  .sorted().toList();
Collectors.groupingBy(Employee::dept,
  Collectors.averagingDouble(Employee::salary));

Ops: filter, map, flatMap(1→many), distinct, sorted, limit · Terminal: collect, toList, reduce, count, anyMatch. Stream = single-use, no side effects.

A4Functional interfaces

Function<T,R>apply · transform · .map()
Predicate<T>test · boolean · .filter()
Consumer<T>accept · void · .forEach()
Supplier<T>get · produce · orElseGet()

Method refs: Integer::parseInt, System.out::println, String::trim, ArrayList::new.

A3HashMap — the answer

put/get: ① hashCode() → ② index = hash & (n-1) → ③ walk bucket, compare with equals().

Contract: equal objects ⇒ equal hashCode, else get() never finds it. Use immutable keys (String, records). HashMap not thread-safe → ConcurrentHashMap. Default cap 16, load 0.75.

P3Virtual threads (Java 21)

JVM-managed, ~free; run millions. Block on I/O ⇒ unmount from carrier OS thread. Simple blocking code scales like async.

Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor()

Help: I/O-bound. Not: CPU-bound. Don’t pool them; avoid synchronized around blocking calls (pinning). Spring Boot 3.2+: spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true.

A5REST methods & status

GETread · safe · idempotent POSTcreate · not idempotent PUT/PATCHreplace / partial · idempotent DELETEremove · idempotent

2xx ok (200,201,204) · 4xx client (400,401,403,404) · 5xx server (500,503).

P4Spring Boot essentials

Layers: @RestController → @Service (@Transactional) → @Repository → DB.

DI: container creates beans, injects them. Prefer constructor injection (final, testable).

@RestController // + @GetMapping/@PostMapping
@Service // business logic
interface R extends JpaRepository<Customer,Long>{
  List<Customer> findByActiveTrue(); //derived }

Errors: @RestControllerAdvice+@ExceptionHandler→status+body. Validate: @Valid + @NotBlank/@Email (needs validation starter). DTOs, never expose entities. Watch N+1 → JOIN FETCH.

P4Security (stateless)

JWT = signed token (header.payload.signature) carrying claims; server verifies signature, no session. OAuth2/OIDC = how tokens are issued. Keycloak = identity provider. Config a SecurityFilterChain bean (no more WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter in Security 6).

P5Spring Batch = ETL in code

SourceItemReader
TransformItemProcessor (return null = filter)
TargetItemWriter
MappingStep (chunk = commit interval)
WorkflowJob · run log = BATCH_* tables

Chunk: read N→process→write N→commit→repeat; restartable. Skip/retry = faultTolerant().skip(...). Batch 5: JobBuilder/StepBuilder + JobRepository.

A7SQL fast retrieval

Leftmost-prefix: index (symbol,date) serves WHERE symbol=? & both, not date alone.

Ignored when: low selectivity, stale stats (ANALYZE), function on column, leading LIKE '%x'. Covering index (INCLUDE) answers from index. Index high-cardinality cols.

GOSetup & posture

java -version  # 21
mvn -version
# scaffold: start.spring.io  Web+JPA+Validation+Batch
docker compose up -d   # postgres
mvn spring-boot:run

Honest line: “My Java is pre-8 vintage; I’m ramping fast on Spring Boot. My depth is ETL, SQL, and event-driven systems — Spring Batch is my Informatica/Pentaho experience in code.” Lead with strength, be candid on gaps.
